# Project Knowledge

# Sigil — Self-Knowledge

## What Sigil Is
An AI company orchestrator — 9 Rust crates, 28 CLI commands, web dashboard at entity.business.
You tell it what you want, it routes to the right agents and executes via Claude Code workers.
Character: Rei (零), the living Sigil, multi-archetype personality.

## Crates
- `sigil-cli` — CLI binary
- `sigil-core` — Config, traits, agent loop, identity
- `sigil-orchestrator` — Daemon, supervisor, worker, chat engine, conversation store, dispatch, audit, expertise, blackboard, preflight, decomposition, failure analysis
- `sigil-web` — Axum REST API + WebSocket (JWT auth, IPC proxy to daemon)
- `sigil-tasks` — Task DAG (JSONL), missions, dependency inference
- `sigil-memory` — SQLite + FTS5 + vector embeddings (OpenRouter), hybrid search with MMR reranking
- `sigil-providers` — OpenRouter, Anthropic, Ollama + cost estimation
- `sigil-gates` — Telegram, Discord, Slack channels
- `sigil-tools` — Shell, file, git, tasks, delegate, skills, porkbun

## Message Flow
```
User → Chat (web/telegram) → ChatEngine
  Quick path: intents (create task, note, close) + memory-enriched status
  Full path: task created → supervisor → worker → Claude Code → reflection → memory
```

## Memory Architecture
- Per-project SQLite DBs at projects/{name}/.sigil/memory.db
- Hybrid search: BM25 keyword (40%) + vector cosine similarity (60%) + MMR reranking
- Categories: fact, procedure, preference, context, evergreen
- Scopes: domain (project-wide), system (cross-project), entity (agent-specific)
- Write path: worker reflection + chat notes + lifecycle consolidation
- Read path: pre-execution enrichment (top 10 memories injected into identity)

## Lock Architecture (CRITICAL)
- IPC handlers use try_lock() on task boards — never block waiting for patrol
- Patrol loop acquires supervisor locks in parallel via join_all
- list_project_summaries() snapshots project list first, releases RwLocks

## Key Learnings
- Claude Code 2.1.81 requires --verbose with --output-format stream-json
- Reflection model must use OpenRouter default (minimax), not project model
- Agent name must differ from project name (registry overwrites)
- Expertise ledger accumulates failures → deprioritizes agents → reset when needed
- Cron tasks accumulate when workers can't execute — purge stale ones periodically

## Infrastructure
- Server: 5.9.83.245 (Ubuntu 24.04)
- Services: sigil-daemon.service, sigil-web.service (port 8400)
- Nginx: entity.business → static SPA + /api proxy
- DNS: Porkbun (entity.business, algostaking.com, riftdecks.shop, aeqi.io, entity.legal)
- Databases: SQLite (audit, blackboard, expertise, dispatches, memory), PostgreSQL (algostaking)

- [worker] synthesis:paperclip-ux-deep: # Paperclip UX Deep Synthesis — Visual Design + Interaction Patterns

## WHAT MAKES IT FEEL POLISHED (the 12 principles)

1. **Systematic design restraint** — No shadows, no textures, no gradients. Pure flat with subtle borders. Sharp corners (radius=0) with rounded-full badges for contrast.
2. **Breathing room** — Generous padding (py-3 to py-5), gaps (gap-2.5), makes content spacious not cramped.
3. **Type scale harmony** — text-[13px] nav, text-sm body, text-xs metadata. Deliberate sizing creates hierarchy without jumps.
4. **Semantic color everywhere** — Status colors from single source (status-colors.ts). Green=done, red=blocked, yellow=progress, blue=todo. Applied consistently in badges, dots, borders.
5. **Bouncy easing** — cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) on key transitions. Feels energetic but not frenetic.
6. **Opacity layering** — bg-accent/50 for hover, /30 for selected, /80 for text dimming. Avoids hard color jumps.
7. **Live indicators done right** — 2-layer pulse (animate-ping at 75% opacity + solid dot on top). Cyan for running.
8. **Click targets matter** — Min 44px height on touch devices. Entire rows clickable, not just text.
9. **Auto-hide scrollbars** — Transparent until hover. Reduces visual noise.
10. **Zero decoration** — No icons in empty space. No gratuitous borders. Every pixel earns its place.
11. **Keyboard shortcuts visible** — Shown in command palette results and menu items, right-aligned in muted text.
12. **prefers-reduced-motion respected** — All animations disabled for accessibility.

## SPECIFIC PATTERNS TO ADOPT FOR SIGIL-UI

### Sidebar
- **Section collapsing**: Chevron rotates 90deg, content collapses with animation
- **Live run indicators**: Pulsing cyan dot + "X live" text next to agents
- **Alert dots**: Red circle with 2px background-color shadow ring (pops off dark bg)
- **Nav active state**: Full bg-accent fill (not just border/text change)
- **Text sizing**: 13px for nav items (slightly smaller than body, reduces visual weight)
- **Budget pause markers**: Red circle with dollar icon on paused projects/agents

### Properties Panel (Right Side)
- **Width**: 320px with transition-[width,opacity] 200ms ease-in-out
- **Toggle**: Keyboard shortcut (]) + button
- **Content-driven**: PanelContext provider — openPanel(content), closePanel()
- **Hidden on mobile**: md:flex (desktop only)

### Activity Stream
- **Entry animation**: translateY(-14px) → overshoot to +2px → settle at 0. 520ms. With 4px blur fade.
- **Highlight**: Left accent border + bg-accent that fades over 920ms on new items.
- **Row structure**: Actor identity + action verb + entity name + time-ago right-aligned.

### Agent Detail Tabs
- **Desktop**: TabsList horizontal
- **Mobile**: Falls back to native <select> dropdown (PageTabBar pattern)
- **Tab content**: Scrollable independently from tab bar

### Forms & Dialogs
- **Progressive disclosure**: Default simple, "Advanced" link reveals more
- **Draft persistence**: localStorage with 800ms debounce autosave
- **Dirty tracking**: Overlay pattern (original vs edits), sticky save button appears when dirty
- **Cmd+Enter to submit**: Standard across all forms
- **Inline editing**: Click text → contenteditable div → blur/Enter commits → Escape reverts
- **Autosave indicator**: "Autosaving..." → "Saved" (green, fades after 1.5s)

### Schedule/Trigger Editor
- **7 presets**: Every minute, every hour, daily, weekdays, weekly, monthly, custom
- **Smart detection**: Parses existing cron back to preset + settings
- **Adaptive UI**: Time inputs only shown for relevant presets
- **Day selection**: Toggle buttons for weekly, dropdown for monthly
- **Human-readable description**: "Weekdays at 2:30 PM" generated from cron

### Loading & Empty States
- **Skeletons**: Match final layout proportions (no shimmer, solid grey)
- **Empty**: Centered icon (40x40, muted bg) + message + CTA button
- **Generous padding**: py-16 for empty state vertical centering

## WHAT SIGIL SHOULD DO DIFFERENTLY (not copy)

### Keep Our Dark Theme
Paperclip is light-first with dark mode. Sigil is OLED-black (#0a0a0a) with bronze accent (#c9a050). This is distinctive. Don't switch to neutral grey.
- Keep: radial gradients, glow effects, accent-glow shadows
- Keep: JetBrains Mono + Inter + Space Grotesk (Paperclip uses system fonts)
- Keep: Glass effects on login card

### Keep Our Rounded Aesthetic
Paperclip uses radius=0 (sharp corners). Sigil uses radius-md/lg. Our rounded corners with the dark theme create a softer, more premium feel. Don't flatten.

### Keep CSS Custom Properties
Paperclip uses Tailwind utility classes. Sigil uses CSS custom properties in tokens.css. Our approach gives consistent theming without Tailwind's class verbosity. Both work, no need to switch.

### Keep Streaming Chat as Primary
Paperclip has no chat. Their primary interaction is task assignment. Sigil's primary interaction is conversational. The chat panel should remain the default view, not an afterthought.

## MAPPING PAPERCLIP → SIGIL CONCEPTS

| Paperclip | Sigil Equivalent | Notes |
|-----------|-----------------|-------|
| Company | (single operator) | Skip multi-tenant |
| Project | Project | Same concept |
| Agent | Persistent Agent | Sigil agents have memory + triggers |
| Issue | Task | Sigil tasks are simpler (no sub-issues, no labels) |
| Routine | Trigger | Sigil triggers are more mature |
| Run | Session/Execution | Sigil streams events, Paperclip stores transcripts |
| Approval | Permission "ask" | Sigil needs web UI for this |
| Company Skill | Skill (.toml) | Sigil skills have tool restrictions |
| Execution Workspace | Worktree | Similar concept, different management |
| Heartbeat | Daemon patrol loop | Sigil is event-driven, not polling |
| Adapter | Provider | Sigil owns the runtime, not wrapping external tools |
| Budget Policy | (needs building) | Per-scope with warn + hard stop |
| Activity Log | Audit trail | Already exists |
| Inbox | (needs building) | For approval/clarification queue |

- [system] orchestration-redesign-v6: Orchestration Redesign v6 (2026-04-02). Full spec at docs/orchestration-redesign.md. Phase 1 complete (sg-038).

**Core principle**: Tasks don't find agents. Agents create tasks.

**4 primitives**:
1. Agent — persistent identity (UUID, system_prompt, department_id, triggers, memory)
2. Department — UUID org unit (name, manager_id, parent_id hierarchy). Replaces parent_id on agents.
3. Task — always agent-bound (agent_id required), never free-floating
4. Delegation — unified delegate tool for ALL inter-agent interaction

**Unified delegate tool** replaces dispatch_send + delegate + project_assign + channel_post:
```
delegate(to, prompt, response, create_task, skill, tools)
```
5 response modes: origin (inject back into calling session), perpetual (into agent's perpetual session), async (spawn fresh session for sender), department (post to dept ConversationStore), none (fire and forget).

**Department schema**:
```sql
departments (id UUID PK, name TEXT, project TEXT, manager_id UUID NULL FK agents, parent_id UUID NULL FK departments)
agents (... department_id UUID NULL FK departments, NO parent_id ...)
```
Manager is IN the parent department, MANAGES the child department. Escalation follows dept.parent_id chain. Blackboard scoped by dept UUID. ConversationStore keyed by dept UUID.

**Cascade prevention**: department channel writes have emit_event flag. Initiating messages emit events (triggers fire). Agent responses store but don't emit (no cascade loop).

**Approval**: existing ask_clarification tool + ClarificationMiddleware, rewired to route via department chain instead of hardcoded escalation_target.

**Shadow as inbox**: root department manager. All unresolved escalations surface in Shadow's perpetual session → user.

**Phase 1 DONE** (sg-038): removed AgentRole enum, 6 unused DispatchKind variants, gutted select_agent_for_task(), removed TeamConfig.agents/router_model. -263 lines.

**Remaining phases**: 2 (departments table), 3 (agent-bound tasks), 4 (agent worker pool), 5 (unified delegate), 6 (dept escalation), 7 (blackboard scoping), 8 (rewire clarification), 9 (dept broadcast).
- [domain] agent-orchestration-implemented: Persistent agent orchestration implemented (2026-04-01). Branch: sg-agent-orchestration, merged to sg-022/context-pipeline.

**Org tree**: parent_id field on PersistentAgent (FK, ON DELETE SET NULL). Tree queries: parent(), children(), siblings(), department_name(), set_parent(). Department = leader + direct reports, derived from parent_id. Shadow = root (parent_id: None).

**Communication primitives**:
- 1:1 directed: dispatch_bus.send() → DispatchReceived event → target agent's trigger fires
- 1:many broadcast: channel_post tool → ChannelMessage event → department agents' triggers fire
- Conversation context: ConversationStore with department_chat_id() for persistent channels

**New tools injected for all persistent agents** (in supervisor.create_worker()):
- dispatch_read + dispatch_send (from MailReadTool/MailSendTool)
- channel_post (ChannelPostTool — posts to ConversationStore + emits ChannelMessage event)

**Org context injection**: supervisor queries parent/siblings/children and injects into worker identity as "## Your Organization" block (manager, peers, reports).

**New skills**: process-dispatch.toml, process-channel-message.toml, orchestration-guide.toml.

**Event system**: DispatchReceived emitted from dispatch_bus.send() (EventBroadcaster wired into DispatchBus). ChannelMessage emitted from ChannelPostTool.

**Agent template example**:
```yaml
name: engineer
parent: shadow
triggers:
  - name: on-dispatch
    event: dispatch_received
    cooldown_secs: 60
    skill: process-dispatch
  - name: on-channel
    event: channel_message
    cooldown_secs: 120
    skill: process-channel-message
```

606 tests passing.
- [domain] claude-code-orchestration-implementation-details: Claude Code orchestration implementation details (2026-03-31). From 119K-token deep analysis of runAgent.ts, agentToolUtils.ts, forkSubagent.ts, resumeAgent.ts, SendMessageTool.ts, toolOrchestration.ts, toolExecution.ts, inProcessRunner.ts, task types.

SUBAGENT STATE ISOLATION (createSubagentContext):
| Field | Default | Override |
| readFileState | Cloned LRU | overrides.readFileState |
| abortController | New child (linked) | shareAbortController |
| setAppState | No-op () => {} | shareSetAppState |
| setAppStateForTasks | ALWAYS parent's root store | never isolated |
| contentReplacementState | Cloned from parent | overrides |
| localDenialTracking | Fresh state | inherited if shareSetAppState |
| queryTracking | New chain ID, depth+1 | - |
| UI callbacks | All undefined | - |

CRITICAL: setAppStateForTasks always reaches root store even when setAppState is no-op. Prevents zombie processes (background bash tasks registered/killed properly).

TOOL EXECUTION 5-PHASE PIPELINE (toolExecution.ts):
Phase 1 — Input Validation: Zod parse → tool.validateInput()
Phase 2 — Pre-Tool Hooks: speculative classifier start (parallel) + PreToolUse hooks (parallel). Hook results: message, hookPermissionResult, hookUpdatedInput, preventContinuation, stopReason, stop, additionalContext
Phase 3 — Permission Check: resolveHookPermissionDecision → canUseTool merge → PermissionDenied hooks on classifier denial (may allow retry)
Phase 4 — Tool Execution: tool.call() with progress callback + session activity timer
Phase 5 — Post-Tool Processing: processToolResultBlock (content replacement) → PostToolUse hooks → git commit tracking → contextModifier application

TOOL BATCHING ALGORITHM (toolOrchestration.ts):
```
for each tool_use in order:
  parse input with Zod
  call tool.isConcurrencySafe(parsedInput) (catch exceptions → not safe)
  if safe AND current batch is safe: append to batch
  else: start new batch
```
Result: alternating batches of concurrent-safe and exclusive.
Concurrent batches: join_all with maxConcurrency=10, context modifiers QUEUED then applied in order after batch
Exclusive batches: sequential, context modifiers applied IMMEDIATELY after each tool

ASYNC AGENT LIFECYCLE (runAsyncAgentLifecycle):
1. Create ProgressTracker, start periodic summarization via onCacheSafeParams
2. Iterate query() messages → push to agentMessages, update AppState task progress, emit SDK events
3. Finalize via finalizeAgentTool() (extract last assistant text, count tokens/tools)
4. Mark task COMPLETED first (before network ops like worktree/classifier)
5. Optional: classifyHandoffIfNeeded() — post-agent YOLO classifier on transcript
6. Get worktree result
7. Enqueue XML task-notification

Error: AbortError → killAsyncAgent → 'killed' notification. Other → failAsyncAgent → 'failed' notification.
Cleanup: clearInvokedSkillsForAgent, clearDumpState

SEND MESSAGE ROUTING:
1. Look up in agentNameRegistry (friendly name → agent ID)
2. Try raw agent ID format
3. If in AppState tasks as LocalAgentTask:
   - Running: queuePendingMessage() → drained at tool-round boundaries
   - Stopped: resumeAgentBackground() from disk transcript + new message
4. If evicted: try resumeAgentBackground() from disk
5. Fallback: ambient team mailbox system

AGENT RESUME (resumeAgent.ts):
1. Read transcript + metadata concurrently
2. Sanitize: filterWhitespaceOnly → filterOrphanedThinking → filterUnresolvedToolUses
3. Reconstruct contentReplacementState from sidechain records
4. Validate worktree (exists? bump mtime to prevent cleanup)
5. Resolve agent definition (fork → FORK_AGENT, named → find in activeAgents, fallback → GENERAL_PURPOSE)
6. Reconstruct system prompt (prefer rendered bytes, fallback → rebuild)
7. Register task, spawn async lifecycle
8. Return immediately with {agentId, description, outputFile}

FORK CACHE SHARING (buildForkedMessages):
1. Clone the assistant message (all tool_use, thinking, text preserved)
2. For EVERY tool_use: create tool_result with IDENTICAL placeholder "Fork started -- processing in background"
3. Create single user message: [...toolResultBlocks, directiveTextBlock]
4. Only the final text block differs per child → maximizes cache hits
5. Anti-recursion: scan messages for <fork-boilerplate> tag, also check querySource

IN-PROCESS TEAMMATE LIFECYCLE:
- Main loop: while !aborted && !shouldExit
- Per-turn: create work abort controller (Escape stops turn not teammate)
- Two-level abort: abortController (lifecycle) vs currentWorkAbortController (per-turn)
- Idle polling: every 500ms check pendingUserMessages > shutdown > team-lead > peer > task claims
- Messages capped at TEAMMATE_MESSAGES_UI_CAP=50 in AppState (full history local + disk)
- Permission bridge: ask → queue into leader's UI dialog with worker badge, Promise resolves on allow/reject/abort

NOTIFICATION DEDUP: All paths atomically check-and-set task.notified flag. If already set (e.g., by TaskStop), skip.

PERMISSION MODE RESOLUTION FOR SUBAGENTS:
- Agent's own permissionMode overrides parent UNLESS parent is bypassPermissions/acceptEdits/auto
- Async agents that can't show UI: shouldAvoidPermissionPrompts=true (auto-deny after hooks)
- Async agents with UI (bubble, in-process): awaitAutomatedChecksBeforeDialog=true (classifier first)

SIGIL IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
1. Our DelegateTool needs the 2-level abort pattern (task lifecycle vs current work)
2. Need agent name registry for friendly-name → ID routing in ContinueAgentTool
3. Tool batching algorithm should use the partition-then-execute pattern (not our current flat safe/unsafe split)
4. Post-agent classifier (classifyHandoffIfNeeded) is valuable for autonomous safety
5. Content replacement state must be CLONED to subagents (not fresh) for cache consistency
6. Notification dedup via atomic flag prevents duplicate deliveries on kill+complete race

CORE INSIGHT: Paperclip is a MANAGEMENT LAYER that delegates to external LLMs (10 adapters). Express.js + PostgreSQL, 47 tables, polling-based heartbeat. Sigil IS the execution engine — orchestrator owns the runtime. This is the moat.

ADOPT (7 items):

SKIP: multi-tenant, PostgreSQL, adapter abstraction, plugin system, BetterAuth, 47-table schema, heartbeat polling, worker threads.

SIGIL AHEAD ON: entity memory (no Paperclip equivalent), trigger+skill architecture (their routines are Beta), middleware chain (they have none), streaming chat (they have no chat), code graph, blackboard scoping.

- [system] sg-011-pipeline-activation: sg-011: Pipeline Activation (2026-03-31). Made pipeline steps visible in existing tool responses instead of requiring extra calls.

Design principle applied: "Don't add more steps — enrich the steps that already happen." The model already calls recall, create_task, close_task, blackboard post. Make those responses carry pipeline context.

4 changes:

1. **sigil_recall enriched**: Response now includes `skills_available` array listing all skill names for the project + shared skills. The model sees available skills every time it recalls without calling sigil_skills(list) separately.

2. **sigil_create_task enriched**: Response includes `agents_available` (by phase: "discover: researcher", "verify: reviewer", etc.) and `next_steps` (3 concrete tool calls to start the pipeline). The model gets a pipeline kickstart in the same response that gives it the task ID.

3. **sigil_close_task enriched**: Response checks if `task:{id}:review` exists on the blackboard. If not, includes `review_warning` with a ready-to-copy sigil_delegate command. The model can't close a task without seeing "No review posted — consider: sigil_delegate(agent='reviewer')."

4. **PostToolUse on blackboard plan post**: nudge-delegation.sh detects when a plan is posted (key contains ":plan") and outputs: "Plan posted. Before implementing, consider: sigil_delegate(agent='reviewer', project, task_id, prompt='Review this plan.')" Triggers only on plan posts, not all blackboard writes.

36 hooks total (was 35). 12 MCP tools with enriched responses. The pipeline is now embedded in the tool responses the model already reads — no extra steps, no extra friction.
- [domain] cc-parity-implementation-plan-v2: CC ↔ Sigil parity implementation plan v2 (2026-03-31). 5 phases, 19-24 days estimated.

CURRENT DELTA SUMMARY:
- Sigil AHEAD in: memory (SQLite+FTS5+vector vs flat .md), multi-agent (Supervisor+Router+Workers vs prompt-only coordinator), code intelligence (sigil-graph vs nothing), tasks (DAG with missions vs flat registry), persistent agents (registry+entity memory vs sessions only), cost control (budget ledger vs basic tracking)
- CC AHEAD in: context pipeline (6 stages vs 2), streaming tool execution, mid-turn enrichment (30+ attachment types), subagent lifecycle (async/fork/continue), tool interface richness (~40 methods vs 6), permission system (classifier, 3-way, multi-racer), hook events (27 vs 6), post-compact restoration
- AT PARITY: compaction prompt (9-section), tool concurrency (safe/exclusive partition), session resume, output recovery, diminishing returns, fallback model

PHASE 1 — Context Pipeline (agent.rs, ~500 lines, 3-4 days):
1a. Snip compaction (remove whole rounds, no API call)
1b. Microcompact (clear old tool results by count, keep recent N)
1c. Reactive compaction (413 recovery → compact → retry)
1d. Post-compact restoration (skills, plan, tool deltas)
1e. State machine transition variants (ReactiveCompact, StopHookBlocking, TokenBudgetContinuation)

PHASE 2 — Subagent Lifecycle (delegate.rs + new, ~800 lines, 5-6 days):
2a. Async delegation (tokio::spawn, return agent_id immediately)
2b. Agent continuation (ContinueAgentTool, queue to running / resume stopped)
2c. Notification protocol (XML task-notification injected via collect_attachments)
2d. Fork cache sharing (CacheSafeParams — match parent's system prompt + tools + model)

PHASE 3 — Streaming Tool Execution (agent.rs + providers, ~600 lines, 4-5 days):
3a. StreamingToolExecutor struct (add_tool during stream, concurrent-safe parallel, exclusive queue)
3b. Provider streaming (SSE chunk stream, feed tool_use blocks incrementally)

PHASE 4 — Mid-Turn Enrichment + Tool Interface (agent.rs + traits.rs, ~400 lines, 3-4 days):
4a. Enrichment pipeline (file changes, memory prefetch, notifications, skill suggestions)
4b. Tool trait enrichment (is_destructive, interrupt_behavior, max_result_size, should_defer, activity_description)

PHASE 5 — Observer Expansion + Shell Hooks (observer.rs + new, ~600 lines, 4-5 days):
5a. 15+ new Observer methods (pre/post_compact, subagent start/stop, permission, file_changed, task events)
5b. Shell hook executor (env-file input, JSON stdout, exit code semantics: 0=ok, 2=block)

EXECUTION: Phase 1 first (unblocks everything), Phase 2 second (multi-agent parity), Phases 3-5 parallelizable. Each phase = separate task (sg-021 through sg-025), own branch.

THESIS: After all 5 phases, Sigil native runtime = CC-grade context management + Sigil's superior orchestration, memory, persistence. The unified orchestrator+harness runtime.
- [system] architecture-v5-complete: Sigil Architecture v5 — Complete System (2026-03-31)

## What Sigil Is
Agent runtime + multi-agent orchestration engine + persistent agent platform. Rust. 10 crates. Replaces Claude Code's agent loop with a superior architecture while currently using Claude Code as the primary development tool (Path A/B strategy).

## The Agent Model

**Persistent agents** live in a SQLite registry (~/.sigil/agents.db). Each has: UUID (stable identity), name (display label, not unique), system_prompt (from frontmatter template), project/department scope, capabilities, model preference. Created once via `sigil agent spawn template.md`. Multiple agents can share a name (board of 5 shadows).

- [domain] claude-code-coordinator-mode-reference: Claude Code coordinator mode (reference for Sigil Supervisor improvement). Source: /tmp/claude-agent-sdk/src/coordinator/coordinatorMode.ts

Coordinator mode is enabled by CLAUDE_CODE_COORDINATOR_MODE=1. The entire behavior is a 350-line system prompt, NOT code logic.

COORDINATOR TOOLS: Agent (spawn worker), SendMessage (continue worker), TaskStop (stop worker)

COORDINATOR PROMPT STRUCTURE:
1. Role — "You are a coordinator. Help user, direct workers, synthesize results."
2. Tools — Agent, SendMessage, TaskStop mechanics
3. Workers — subagent_type: 'worker', autonomous execution
4. Task Workflow — phases with concurrency rules:
   - Research (parallel workers)
   - Synthesis (coordinator reads findings, crafts specs)
   - Implementation (one at a time per file area)
   - Verification (independent workers, skeptical)
5. Prompt Engineering — the most detailed section:
   - "Workers can't see your conversation. Every prompt must be self-contained."
   - "Never write 'based on your findings' — synthesize yourself"
   - Continue vs spawn decision matrix (context overlap)
   - Good/bad prompt examples
6. Example Session — full multi-turn example

KEY DESIGN PRINCIPLES:
- Coordinator MUST synthesize — never lazy-delegate
- Parallelism is the superpower — fan out research
- Workers are async — launch and report back
- Continue workers whose work is complete (reuse context)
- Scratchpad directory for cross-worker durable knowledge
- Worker results arrive as XML <task-notification> in user messages

SIGIL INSIGHT: Our Supervisor uses code-based routing (expertise matching, load balancing). CC's approach encodes the coordinator's behavior entirely in the prompt, making it more flexible and transparent. The "synthesis" principle is key — the coordinator must understand and restate findings, not just pass them through. Our Supervisor should enforce this pattern.

Worker notification format:
<task-notification>
<task-id>{agentId}</task-id>
<status>completed|failed|killed</status>
<summary>{human-readable status}</summary>
<result>{agent's final text response}</result>
<usage><total_tokens>N</total_tokens><tool_uses>N</tool_uses><duration_ms>N</duration_ms></usage>
</task-notification>

## 1. SPECULATION ENGINE (speculation.ts) — HIGH VALUE
Speculative execution of the predicted next user action. When idle after a turn:
- Generates a "prompt suggestion" (what the user will type next) via a forked Haiku call
- If speculation is enabled, EXECUTES that predicted prompt speculatively in a forked agent
- Uses copy-on-write filesystem overlay: writes go to /tmp overlay dir, reads check overlay first
- Boundaries: stops at non-read-only bash, file edits requiring permission, unknown tools
- On user acceptance: copies overlay files to real FS, injects speculated messages into conversation
- Pipelined: generates NEXT suggestion while current speculation runs
- Tracks time saved per session for metrics
Architecture: forkedAgent + overlay FS + boundary detection + message injection
Sigil relevance: VERY HIGH — this is predictive pre-computation. Could pre-run likely next steps.

## 2. AUTO-DREAM / MEMORY CONSOLIDATION (autoDream.ts, consolidationPrompt.ts) — HIGH VALUE
Background memory consolidation that fires as a forked subagent when:
- Time gate: hours since last consolidation >= minHours (default 24h)
- Session gate: transcript count since last >= minSessions (default 5)
- Lock: no other process mid-consolidation
4-phase prompt: Orient (ls memory dir) → Gather recent signal (grep transcripts) → Consolidate (merge/update/delete) → Prune and index (keep entrypoint under 25KB)
Tool constraints: bash restricted to read-only commands during dream.
Fires from postSamplingHook. Lock file with rollback on failure.
Sigil relevance: HIGH — Sigil already has memory but lacks automated consolidation/pruning cycles.

## 3. MAGIC DOCS (magicDocs.ts, prompts.ts) — MEDIUM-HIGH VALUE
Auto-updating documentation triggered by file reads:
- Files with "# MAGIC DOC: [title]" header are auto-tracked
- Optional italicized instructions line after header for custom update guidance
- Post-sampling hook checks if conversation is idle (no tool calls in last turn)
- Runs forked subagent (Sonnet, Edit-only permissions) to update the doc with new learnings
- Custom prompt templates loadable from ~/.claude/magic-docs/prompt.md
- sequential() wrapper prevents concurrent updates
Sigil relevance: MEDIUM — could auto-maintain project docs, but Sigil's memory system serves similar purpose.

## 4. ADVISOR TOOL (advisor.ts) — HIGH VALUE
Server-side tool backed by a stronger reviewer model:
- Agent calls "advisor" tool → entire conversation forwarded to stronger model
- No parameters needed — advisor sees full context automatically
- Prompt instructs: call BEFORE substantive work, when stuck, when considering approach change, before declaring done
- "Make deliverable durable before calling advisor" — writes first, then consults
- Conflict resolution protocol: if advisor disagrees with evidence, make reconcile call
- Model pair: base model + advisor model (configurable)
Sigil relevance: HIGH — maps to Sigil's delegation pattern. Could have a "review" delegation that sends full context to stronger model.

## 5. PROMPT SUGGESTION (promptSuggestion.ts) — MEDIUM VALUE
Predicts what user will type next after each turn:
- Uses forked agent with full conversation context
- Extensive filter system: blocks evaluative text, Claude-voice, meta-text, too long/short, multiple sentences
- Cache-safe: denies tools via callback (not empty tools array) to preserve prompt cache hit
- Suppression logic: early conversation, rate limits, plan mode, pending permissions
Sigil relevance: MEDIUM — useful for interactive UX but Sigil is primarily non-interactive.

## 6. TOOL USE SUMMARY GENERATOR (toolUseSummaryGenerator.ts) — MEDIUM VALUE
Generates human-readable 30-char summaries of tool batches using Haiku:
- Input: tool names, truncated inputs/outputs, last assistant text for context

TOP 10 PATTERNS TO ADOPT:

1. ANTI-RATIONALIZATION TABLES — Every discipline skill has a table of excuses agents actually used + one-line counters. Captured from pressure testing. Backed by research: Authority + Commitment doubled LLM compliance (33% → 72%).

2. GATE FUNCTIONS — Structured check BEFORE critical actions. verification-before-completion: IDENTIFY → RUN → READ → VERIFY → THEN claim. Should be infrastructure hooks in Sigil, not just prompt discipline.

3. TWO-STAGE REVIEW — Spec compliance FIRST, code quality SECOND. Never reverse. No point polishing code that doesn't meet spec. Maps to two sequential sigil_delegate calls.

4. PIPELINE WITH EXPLICIT TERMINAL STATES — Each skill declares its ONE successor. brainstorming → writing-plans → subagent-driven-development → finishing-branch. No ambiguity.

5. STRUCTURED STATUS PROTOCOL — Implementers report: DONE / DONE_WITH_CONCERNS / NEEDS_CONTEXT / BLOCKED. Controller has specific handling per status. BLOCKED is respected.

6. CSO (Claude Search Optimization) — Skill descriptions must contain ONLY trigger conditions, never workflow summaries. If description summarizes workflow, agent follows description instead of reading full skill.

7. FRESH CONTEXT CURATION — Paste task content inline for subagents. Never file references. Controller curates exactly what each subagent needs. Prevents context pollution.

8. 3-FIX ESCALATION — If 3 fix attempts fail, STOP and question architecture. Don't attempt #4 without fundamentally reconsidering approach.

9. PRESSURE TESTING FOR SKILLS — RED: run without skill, capture failures. GREEN: write skill addressing failures. REFACTOR: close loopholes. Combine 3+ pressure types (time, sunk cost, authority, exhaustion).

10. PERMISSION TO FAIL — "It is always OK to say 'this is too hard for me.' Bad work is worse than no work." BLOCKED is valid and respected.

- Dynamic trigger resolution via MCP (vs static description matching)
- Persistent memory across sessions (Superpowers has none)
- Blackboard for cross-skill state (vs file artifacts only)

- Agent registry with capability matching (vs fresh subagents each time)
- Verification as infrastructure hooks (vs prompt-only discipline)

KEY ARCHITECTURAL INSIGHT: Superpowers solves agent discipline entirely through prompt engineering. Sigil can implement the SAME discipline at the infrastructure level — verification gates as hooks, status protocols as type constraints, pipeline ordering as graph dependencies. Prompt engineering for what can't be mechanically enforced. Infrastructure for what can.

- [system] design-principles-harness: Design principles for the Claude Code + Sigil productivity harness. Derived from dogfooding across 7 tasks.

1. ENFORCEMENT > INSTRUCTION. The recall gate (enforced via hook) has 100% compliance. The pipeline workflow (instructed via primer) has ~50%. If a behavior matters, enforce it with a hook. If it's optional, it won't happen under cognitive load.

2. EVENT-DRIVEN > TIME-BASED. Never use timers, TTLs, or "recently" checks. Ask: "what event indicates the model needs X?" Session end, project switch, compaction — these are events. "90 minutes" is an arbitrary number.

3. CODE BLOCKS > PROSE. The primer's pipeline phases with actual tool invocations in code blocks drive behavior. The same information as bullet-point descriptions gets ignored. Models treat code blocks as instructions and prose as context.

4. THE SYSTEM SHOULD VERIFY ITSELF. sigil_graph can check if new code has callers. The graph should be used to validate the graph's own evolution. "Tests pass" proves isolation, not integration.

5. PROACTIVE > REACTIVE. Auto-context on edit, auto-index on session start, auto-nudge on session end. The best features are ones the agent doesn't have to remember to call.

6. TOOLS THAT FEED EACH OTHER > TOOLS THAT STAND ALONE. Memory + graph + skills + blackboard are powerful individually. They're transformative when wired: recall gate hints about graph, compaction preserves skill names, edits trigger re-indexing, communities generate skills.

7. ONE TOOL CALL > FOUR MANUAL STEPS. sigil_delegate (not yet built) should be one call that loads agent template + reads blackboard + spawns subagent + posts results. Currently, delegation requires 4 manual steps — too much friction, so it never happens.

8. FIRST SESSIONS ARE BEST. Pipeline compliance degrades as context compacts. Design for the reality that tasks 5+ in a session will have degraded workflow compliance. Keep critical instructions in the shortest form. Front-load the most important work.

9. BUILD AND USE ARE DIFFERENT. Building a tool and using it require different mindsets. After building sigil_graph, I never used it on my own work. The primer should explicitly say: "if you just built or modified a tool, use it immediately on the code you just wrote."

10. CLEAN OVER CLEVER. jq over python. awk over python. SQLite over custom formats. Shell scripts over Rust for hooks (fast to iterate). Rust for graph processing (performance matters). Match the tool to the problem's actual needs.

SUBAGENT STATE ISOLATION (createSubagentContext):
| Field | Default | Override |
| readFileState | Cloned LRU | overrides.readFileState |
| abortController | New child (linked) | shareAbortController |
| setAppState | No-op () => {} | shareSetAppState |
| setAppStateForTasks | ALWAYS parent's root store | never isolated |
| contentReplacementState | Cloned from parent | overrides |
| localDenialTracking | Fresh state | inherited if shareSetAppState |
| queryTracking | New chain ID, depth+1 | - |
| UI callbacks | All undefined | - |

CRITICAL: setAppStateForTasks always reaches root store even when setAppState is no-op. Prevents zombie processes (background bash tasks registered/killed properly).

TOOL EXECUTION 5-PHASE PIPELINE (toolExecution.ts):
Phase 1 — Input Validation: Zod parse → tool.validateInput()
Phase 2 — Pre-Tool Hooks: speculative classifier start (parallel) + PreToolUse hooks (parallel). Hook results: message, hookPermissionResult, hookUpdatedInput, preventContinuation, stopReason, stop, additionalContext
Phase 3 — Permission Check: resolveHookPermissionDecision → canUseTool merge → PermissionDenied hooks on classifier denial (may allow retry)
Phase 4 — Tool Execution: tool.call() with progress callback + session activity timer
Phase 5 — Post-Tool Processing: processToolResultBlock (content replacement) → PostToolUse hooks → git commit tracking → contextModifier application

TOOL BATCHING ALGORITHM (toolOrchestration.ts):
```
for each tool_use in order:
  parse input with Zod
  call tool.isConcurrencySafe(parsedInput) (catch exceptions → not safe)
  if safe AND current batch is safe: append to batch
  else: start new batch
```
Result: alternating batches of concurrent-safe and exclusive.
Concurrent batches: join_all with maxConcurrency=10, context modifiers QUEUED then applied in order after batch
Exclusive batches: sequential, context modifiers applied IMMEDIATELY after each tool

ASYNC AGENT LIFECYCLE (runAsyncAgentLifecycle):
1. Create ProgressTracker, start periodic summarization via onCacheSafeParams
2. Iterate query() messages → push to agentMessages, update AppState task progress, emit SDK events
3. Finalize via finalizeAgentTool() (extract last assistant text, count tokens/tools)
4. Mark task COMPLETED first (before network ops like worktree/classifier)
5. Optional: classifyHandoffIfNeeded() — post-agent YOLO classifier on transcript
6. Get worktree result
7. Enqueue XML task-notification

Error: AbortError → killAsyncAgent → 'killed' notification. Other → failAsyncAgent → 'failed' notification.
Cleanup: clearInvokedSkillsForAgent, clearDumpState

SEND MESSAGE ROUTING:
1. Look up in agentNameRegistry (friendly name → agent ID)
2. Try raw agent ID format
3. If in AppState tasks as LocalAgentTask:
   - Running: queuePendingMessage() → drained at tool-round boundaries
   - Stopped: resumeAgentBackground() from disk transcript + new message
4. If evicted: try resumeAgentBackground() from disk
5. Fallback: ambient team mailbox system

AGENT RESUME (resumeAgent.ts):
1. Read transcript + metadata concurrently
2. Sanitize: filterWhitespaceOnly → filterOrphanedThinking → filterUnresolvedToolUses
3. Reconstruct contentReplacementState from sidechain records
4. Validate worktree (exists? bump mtime to prevent cleanup)
5. Resolve agent definition (fork → FORK_AGENT, named → find in activeAgents, fallback → GENERAL_PURPOSE)
6. Reconstruct system prompt (prefer rendered bytes, fallback → rebuild)
7. Register task, spawn async lifecycle
8. Return immediately with {agentId, description, outputFile}

FORK CACHE SHARING (buildForkedMessages):
1. Clone the assistant message (all tool_use, thinking, text preserved)
2. For EVERY tool_use: create tool_result with IDENTICAL placeholder "Fork started -- processing in background"
3. Create single user message: [...toolResultBlocks, directiveTextBlock]
4. Only the final text block differs per child → maximizes cache hits
5. Anti-recursion: scan messages for <fork-boilerplate> tag, also check querySource

IN-PROCESS TEAMMATE LIFECYCLE:
- Main loop: while !aborted && !shouldExit
- Per-turn: create work abort controller (Escape stops turn not teammate)
- Two-level abort: abortController (lifecycle) vs currentWorkAbortController (per-turn)
- Idle polling: every 500ms check pendingUserMessages > shutdown > team-lead > peer > task claims
- Messages capped at TEAMMATE_MESSAGES_UI_CAP=50 in AppState (full history local + disk)

NOTIFICATION DEDUP: All paths atomically check-and-set task.notified flag. If already set (e.g., by TaskStop), skip.

PERMISSION MODE RESOLUTION FOR SUBAGENTS:
