content.content: 1 # Orchestration Redesign: Status Quo → Agent-Native Architecture 2 3 ## Table of Contents 4 1. [Status Quo: What Exists Today](#status-quo) 5 2. [What Works Well](#what-works-well) 6 3. [What's Broken or Disconnected](#whats-broken) 7 4. [Design Goal](#design-goal) 8 5. [Proposed Architecture](#proposed-architecture) 9 6. [Migration Path](#migration-path) 10 11 --- 12 13 ## Status Quo: What Exists Today 14 15 ### The Daemon 16 17 Sigil runs as a background daemon (`sigil daemon start`) with a 30-second patrol loop. The daemon owns all shared infrastructure: 18 19 - **ProjectRegistry** — holds all projects and their supervisors 20 - **DispatchBus** — SQLite-backed agent-to-agent message queue with ACK tracking 21 - **CostLedger** — per-task cost tracking, daily budget enforcement (JSONL) 22 - **AuditLog** — SQLite decision trail (routing, assignment, timeout, failure) 23 - **ExpertiseLedger** — SQLite agent success rates by domain (Wilson score ranking) 24 - **Blackboard** — SQLite inter-agent knowledge store (claims, signals, findings, TTL-based) 25 - **ConversationStore** — SQLite chat history, channel messages, timeline events 26 - **AgentRegistry** — SQLite persistent agent identities (UUID, system_prompt, org tree, triggers) 27 - **TriggerStore** — cron/event/once triggers owned by persistent agents 28 - **EventBroadcaster** — pub/sub for real-time execution events 29 - **ChatEngine** — web/Telegram message routing 30 31 IPC via Unix socket at `~/.sigil/rm.sock` (JSON-line protocol, 40+ commands). 32 33 ### The Patrol Loop 34 35 Every 30 seconds: 36 37 ``` 38 1. For each project → Supervisor.patrol() 39 ├─ Reload tasks from disk 40 ├─ Reap finished workers, detect timeouts 41 ├─ Handle blocked tasks (escalation) 42 ├─ Assign ready tasks → spawn AgentWorker (tokio::spawn) 43 └─ Report to leader (PatrolReport dispatch) 44 2. Fire due triggers (cron/event/once) 45 3. Check SIGHUP → hot-reload config 46 4. Persist: dispatches, cost ledger 47 5. Retry unacked dispatches, detect dead letters 48 6. Prune: old costs (7d), expired blackboard, flush memory writes 49 7. Update metrics 50 ``` 51 52 ### The Supervisor (1 per project, 45 fields) 53 54 A Rust struct (`Arc>`) that manages one project's task execution: 55 56 - **Worker pool**: spawn up to `max_workers` concurrent tokio tasks, track timeouts, reap finished 57 - **Agent selection**: `select_agent_for_task()` picks which agent runs a task from the project's team config, using expertise routing + load balancing 58 - **Context injection** (`create_worker()`): loads persistent agent identity, injects blackboard context, org context, skill prompt, domain hints, builds 9-middleware chain 59 - **Escalation**: blocked → retry locally (up to `max_resolution_attempts`) → project leader → system leader → human (Telegram/Discord/Slack) 60 - **Budget enforcement**: check cost ledger before spawning workers 61 - **Verification**: optional post-execution verification pipeline 62 - **Metrics/audit**: record routing decisions, assignments, completions, failures 63 64 The supervisor is NOT an agent. It has no UUID, no system prompt, no memory. It's static Rust code making orchestration decisions. 65 66 ### The Agent Worker (ephemeral task executor) 67 68 Created per-task by `Supervisor.create_worker()`. Fully configured with: 69 70 - Identity (project default + persistent agent override from registry) 71 - Tools (filtered by skill allow/deny lists) 72 - Memory (entity-scoped by agent UUID) 73 - Blackboard context 74 - Org context (manager, peers, reports — injected as markdown) 75 - Skill prompt (from TOML skill file) 76 - 9-middleware chain (loop detection, guardrails, cost tracking, context compression, context budget, memory refresh, clarification, safety net, graph guardrails) 77 - Event broadcaster 78 - Reflection provider (cheap model for post-task insight extraction) 79 80 Execution flow: 81 ``` 82 worker.execute() 83 ├─ middleware.on_start() 84 ├─ Build task context (checkpoints, acceptance criteria, resume brief) 85 ├─ Enrich identity with memory (query planner + entity recall) 86 ├─ Execute agent loop OR Claude Code subprocess 87 │ └─ LLM → tools → LLM → repeat 88 │ ├─ 3-stage compaction (snip → microcompact → full compact) 89 │ ├─ Content replacement for large outputs (>50KB → persist to disk) 90 │ ├─ Notification injection for background subagents 91 │ ├─ Diminishing returns detection (3 turns < 500 tokens → halt) 92 │ └─ Session checkpointing for resume 93 ├─ Fire-and-forget reflection (extract insights → dedup → store with graph edges) 94 ├─ Parse outcome: Done / Blocked / Handoff / Failed 95 │ ├─ Done → checkpoint, close task, dispatch TaskDone 96 │ ├─ Blocked → preserve question, dispatch TaskBlocked 97 │ ├─ Handoff → re-queue with checkpoint (max 3 retries → auto-cancel) 98 │ └─ Failed → LLM failure analysis → classify failure mode → targeted retry/escalate 99 └─ Return (TaskOutcome, RuntimeExecution, cost_usd, turns) 100 ``` 101 102 ### The Agent Loop (crates/sigil-core/src/agent.rs) 103 104 The core LLM conversation engine (~2,200 lines). Runs inside AgentWorker. 105 106 - **Context management**: estimated tokens via `len/4`, compaction at 68% (snip), 80% (full) 107 - **Snip** (free): remove old API rounds, keep head N + tail M messages 108 - **Microcompact** (semi-free): clear old tool results (keep 5 most recent), replace with placeholder 109 - **Full compact** (1 API call): LLM-based structured summary, post-compact file/skill restoration 110 - **Tool execution**: before_tool hook → execute → after_tool hook, concurrent when safe 111 - **Content replacement**: large outputs persisted to `.sigil/persist/{worker}/{id}.txt`, replaced with preview 112 - **Notification drain**: between turns, drain `notification_rx` for background subagent results, inject as User messages 113 - **Perpetual mode**: after EndTurn, wait for next input via `input_rx` instead of breaking 114 - **Session files**: checkpoint at `.sigil/sessions/{task_id}.json` for resume after compaction/crash 115 - **Constants**: max 20 iterations, max 3 compactions per run, diminishing returns at 500 tokens/turn × 3 116 117 ### Subagent Delegation (DelegateTool) 118 119 An agent tool (not a daemon feature) that spawns ephemeral subagents within an agent's runtime. 120 121 **Two modes**: 122 123 - **Sync** (`run_in_background: false`): parent calls `Agent.run(prompt)` and blocks until result 124 - **Async** (`run_in_background: true`): parent spawns `tokio::spawn(Agent.run(prompt))`, continues immediately. Result delivered via `LoopNotification` channel, injected as `` XML between turns 125 126 **Key mechanics**: 127 - Subagent inherits parent's provider, tools (minus delegate tool), identity 128 - Optional tool allowlisting (restrict subagent to specific tools) 129 - Depth-limited: subagents cannot re-delegate (depth > 0 → error) 130 - `AgentInfra` struct holds: `registry: HashMap`, `notification_tx`, `loop_tx` 131 - `AgentHandle` tracks: id, description, status (Running/Completed/Failed), `notified` dedup flag 132 - Atomic notification delivery: update handle status + send notification while holding registry lock 133 134 **Current gap**: `agent_worker.rs execute_agent()` does NOT wire `notification_rx` or create `AgentInfra`. The DelegateTool infrastructure exists but is not connected in the supervisor worker path. It only works when `sigil chat` or `sigil run` sets it up manually. 135 136 ### Persistent Agents (AgentRegistry) 137 138 SQLite registry at `~/.sigil/agents.db`. Each agent has: 139 140 ``` 141 id (UUID) — stable identity, entity memory scope 142 name — human label (NOT unique) 143 display_name — UI display 144 template — origin template name 145 system_prompt — THE AGENT (full personality/instructions) 146 project — optional project scope (None = root/cross-project) 147 department — optional department scope (within project) 148 parent_id — FK to agents.id (org tree) 149 model — preferred LLM 150 capabilities — JSON array (spawn_agents, spawn_projects, manage_triggers) 151 status — Active / Paused / Retired 152 created_at, last_active, session_count, total_tokens 153 color, avatar, faces — TUI visual identity 154 ``` 155 156 **Org tree methods** (all implemented, clean API): 157 - `parent(id)` → manager agent 158 - `children(id)` → direct reports 159 - `siblings(id)` → agents sharing same parent 160 - `department_name(id)` → self name if has children, else parent's name 161 - `set_parent(id, parent_id)` → restructure org chart 162 163 **Template system**: YAML frontmatter in `agents/*/agent.md` → parsed → spawned via `spawn_from_template()` → creates row in agents.db + creates triggers in trigger store. 164 165 ### Triggers 166 167 Owned by persistent agents, stored in agents.db triggers table: 168 169 - **Schedule**: cron expression (e.g., `0 9 * * *`) 170 - **Event**: pattern match on ExecutionEvent + cooldown (TaskCompleted, TaskFailed, ChannelMessage, DispatchReceived, etc.) 171 - **Once**: one-shot at ISO 8601 timestamp, auto-disabled after firing 172 173 When a trigger fires: daemon creates a task with the trigger's skill, bound to the trigger's owning agent. Advance-before-execute pattern prevents duplicate execution on restart. 174 175 ### DispatchBus 176 177 SQLite-backed message queue. Each `Dispatch` has: 178 179 ``` 180 from, to — agent names 181 kind — DispatchKind enum (16 variants) 182 timestamp, first_sent_at 183 read — marked true when recipient calls read() 184 requires_ack — true for critical messages 185 retry_count, max_retries (default 3) 186 ``` 187 188 **DispatchKind variants**: 189 190 | Kind | Used? | Direction | Purpose | 191 |------|-------|-----------|---------| 192 | TaskDone | YES | worker → leader | Task completed | 193 | TaskBlocked | YES | worker → leader | Needs clarification | 194 | TaskFailed | YES | worker → leader | Execution failed | 195 | WorkerCrashed | YES | supervisor → leader | Worker timed out | 196 | Escalation | YES | supervisor → leader | Blocked after max attempts | 197 | Resolution | YES | leader → supervisor | Answer to escalation | 198 | HumanEscalation | YES | supervisor → channels | Terminal escalation | 199 | PatrolReport | GENERATED, NEVER CONSUMED | supervisor → leader | Active/pending counts | 200 | CouncilTopic | NO | — | Never implemented | 201 | CouncilResponse | NO | — | Never implemented | 202 | CouncilSynthesis | NO | — | Never implemented | 203 | AgentAdvice | NO | — | Never sent | 204 | TaskProposal | NO | — | Never sent | 205 | DependencySuggestion | NO | — | Never sent | 206 207 Methods: `send()`, `read(recipient)`, `acknowledge(id)`, `retry_unacked(age)`, `dead_letters()`, `health()`. 208 TTL: 1 hour default. Max queue: 1000 per recipient. Max retries: 3. 209 210 ### Blackboard 211 212 SQLite-backed coordination surface. Each entry has: 213 214 ``` 215 key — identifies entry (e.g., "claim:src/api.rs", "finding:auth-bug") 216 content — free-form text 217 agent — who posted 218 project — scoped to project 219 tags — categorization 220 durability — Transient (24h TTL) or Durable (7d TTL) 221 expires_at — auto-calculated 222 ``` 223 224 **Operations**: post, query (by project + tags), get_by_key, claim (atomic resource lock), release, delete, prune_expired. 225 226 **Scoped visibility** (implemented in `query_scoped()` but NEVER USED in production code paths): 227 - `system:*` → always visible 228 - `project:{name}:*` → only agents in project 229 - `dept:{name}:*` → only agents in department 230 - `agent:{uuid}:*` → only that agent 231 - No prefix → always visible (legacy) 232 233 **Claims**: atomic check/acquire. Same agent → renew. Different agent → Held response. 2h TTL default. 234 235 ### ConversationStore 236 237 SQLite-backed chat history with FTS5 search. Deterministic chat IDs via hashing (project_chat_id, department_chat_id, named_channel_chat_id, agency_chat_id). 238 239 **Dead features**: auto-summarization (threshold 30, never triggered), evict_older_than (never called), timeline events (type exists, only messages recorded), search_transcripts (requires channel_type='transcript', none created). 240 241 ### Config Model 242 243 ```toml 244 [sigil] # name, data_dir, default_runtime 245 [providers.*] # OpenRouter, Anthropic, Ollama + API keys 246 [security] # autonomy, budget limits 247 [memory] # SQLite backend, embedding config 248 [team] # leader, agents[], router_model, max_background_cost 249 [orchestrator] # expertise_routing, preflight, adaptive_retry, etc. 250 [web] # Axum bind, CORS, auth_secret 251 [repos] # Global repo pool (name → path) 252 [[projects]] # name, prefix, repo, model, team, departments, missions 253 ``` 254 255 **PeerAgentConfig** (in sigil.toml `[[agents]]` or `agents/*/agent.toml`): 256 ``` 257 name, prefix, model, runtime, role (AgentRole enum), voice 258 execution_mode, max_workers, max_turns, max_budget_usd 259 default_repo, expertise[], capabilities[], telegram_token_secret 260 ``` 261 262 **AgentRole enum**: Orchestrator (default), Worker, Advisor, Executive — NOT used for routing or behavior differentiation. 263 264 ### Middleware Chain (9 implementations) 265 266 | Middleware | Order | Purpose | 267 |-----------|-------|---------| 268 | Guardrails | 200 | Block dangerous shell ops (rm -rf, DROP TABLE) | 269 | MemoryRefresh | 400 | Re-search memory every N tool calls | 270 | LoopDetection | 500 | MD5 hash sliding window, warn@3, kill@5 repeats | 271 | CostTracking | 600 | Token/cost accumulation, budget ceiling | 272 | ContextBudget | 700 | Cap enrichment at ~200 lines | 273 | GraphGuardrails | — | Block dangerous git ops | 274 | ContextCompression | — | Compress at 50% window, protect first/last | 275 | Clarification | — | Structured ask_clarification, halts execution | 276 | SafetyNet | — | On failure: preserve work artifacts | 277 278 ### Persistence Summary 279 280 | Store | Backend | What's In It | 281 |-------|---------|-------------| 282 | agents.db | SQLite | Persistent agent identities, UUIDs, org tree, triggers | 283 | memory.db | SQLite+FTS5+vectors | Semantic memory per entity (agent UUID scoped) | 284 | audit.db | SQLite | Every routing decision, assignment, timeout, failure | 285 | expertise.db | SQLite | Agent success rates by domain (Wilson score ranking) | 286 | blackboard.db | SQLite | Inter-agent knowledge, resource claims, signals (TTL) | 287 | conversations.db | SQLite | Chat history, channel messages | 288 | dispatches.db | SQLite | Agent-to-agent messages with ACK tracking | 289 | cost_ledger.jsonl | JSONL | Daily per-task cost tracking, budget enforcement | 290 | .tasks/ (per project) | JSONL | Task DAGs with status, dependencies, outcomes | 291 292 --- 293 294 ## What Works Well 295 296 These components are production-quality and should be preserved: 297 298 1. **The patrol loop** — reliable 30s heartbeat, fire-and-forget workers, non-blocking IPC, early wake via Notify 299 2. **Task lifecycle** — Pending → InProgress → Done/Blocked/Failed with checkpoints, retries, escalation 300 3. **Agent loop** — 3-stage compaction, content replacement, session checkpointing, notification injection, diminishing returns detection 301 4. **Middleware chain** — 9 composable behaviors, clean trait with 8 hooks, per-worker instantiation 302 5. **Entity-scoped memory** — persistent agent UUID scopes memory queries, reflection extracts insights, dedup pipeline 303 6. **Trigger system** — cron/event/once with advance-before-execute, auto-disable one-shot, agent-owned 304 7. **Blackboard** — resource claims (atomic), signals, findings with TTL (24h/7d), scoped visibility (implemented) 305 8. **DelegateTool** — sync/async subagent spawning, depth-limited, notification delivery, dedup guard 306 9. **Context injection** (`create_worker()`) — identity from registry, blackboard, org context, skills, tools, middleware 307 10. **Adaptive failure analysis** — LLM-based failure mode classification → targeted retry 308 11. **Preflight assessment** — cheap LLM evaluation before expensive execution 309 12. **Expertise ledger** — Wilson score ranking, deprioritization of failing agents 310 13. **IPC protocol** — 40+ commands, try_lock for non-blocking reads, cursor-based event pagination 311 312 --- 313 314 ## What's Broken or Disconnected 315 316 ### 1. The Org Tree Is Decorative 317 318 `agents.db` has `parent_id` with clean API (parent, children, siblings, department_name, set_parent). But: 319 320 - `select_agent_for_task()` ignores hierarchy — routes by team config + load balance + expertise 321 - Escalation doesn't climb the tree — goes to hardcoded `escalation_target`, not up parent chain 322 - Department field exists but doesn't restrict visibility or assignment 323 - Org context injected as markdown text — agent knows peers but can't act on relationships 324 325 ### 2. Communication Is Vertical-Only 326 327 DispatchBus supports arbitrary from/to addressing and has 16 message types, but only 7 are used — all vertical (worker → leader, leader → supervisor). No agent-to-agent messaging. Agent A cannot send a directed message to Agent B. 328 329 9 dispatch types are dead code (PatrolReport generated but never consumed, Council system never implemented, TaskProposal/DependencySuggestion/AgentAdvice never sent). 330 331 ### 3. The Supervisor Is Not An Agent 332 333 The Supervisor is a Rust struct. It makes routing decisions via static code, not LLM reasoning. No UUID, no memory, no system prompt. Cannot be delegated to or from. All orchestration intelligence is hardcoded. 334 335 ### 4. `select_agent_for_task()` Solves A Non-Problem 336 337 Every task originates from either a trigger (owned by a specific agent) or a delegation decision (by a specific agent). There is no scenario where a task exists without knowing which agent should handle it. The routing logic is solving a problem that doesn't exist. 338 339 ### 5. DelegateTool Is Not Wired In Workers 340 341 The subagent notification infrastructure (AgentInfra, notification channels, LoopNotification drain) exists in delegate.rs and agent.rs, but `agent_worker.rs execute_agent()` does NOT set it up. Workers spawned by the supervisor cannot delegate to subagents. 342 343 ### 6. PeerAgentConfig Conflates Template And Runtime 344 345 Mixes template definition (name, model, expertise) with runtime wiring (telegram_token_secret, default_repo). C-suite agent templates crashed because `role = "executive"` wasn't a valid AgentRole variant. 346 347 ### 7. Channels Overlap With Blackboard 348 349 ConversationStore channels and Blackboard both store scoped text for agent knowledge sharing. Blackboard is strictly more capable (TTL, claims, visibility rules). 350 351 ### 8. Dead / Unused Features 352 353 | Feature | Status | 354 |---------|--------| 355 | AgentRole enum | Not used for routing or behavior | 356 | PatrolReport dispatch | Generated, never consumed | 357 | Council system (3 dispatch types + config) | Zero implementation | 358 | TaskProposal, DependencySuggestion, AgentAdvice | Never sent | 359 | ConversationStore auto-summarization | Never triggered | 360 | `query_cross_project()` on Blackboard | Zero callers | 361 | `query_scoped()` on Blackboard | Implemented, never used | 362 | `department` field | Stored, never enforced | 363 | TeamConfig.router_model | For removed intent classifier | 364 365 --- 366 367 ## Design Goal 368 369 **Tasks don't find agents. Agents create tasks.** 370 371 The entry point is always an agent, never a free-floating task looking for a home. An agent either: 372 1. Handles a task itself (agent loop) 373 2. Spawns an ephemeral subagent (DelegateTool — sync or async) 374 3. Delegates to a child persistent agent (new: dispatch-based) 375 376 No routing decisions. No team matching. No `select_agent_for_task()`. The agent who creates the task already knows who should handle it. 377 378 **The org hierarchy lives on departments, not agents:** 379 - Departments are UUID-identified entities with their own parent/child hierarchy 380 - Agents belong to a department (`department_id`), no `parent_id` on agents 381 - Department managers handle escalation — chain climbs department.parent_id until root 382 - Department members share blackboard scope (findings, signals, claims) 383 384 **Four primitives:** 385 1. **Agent** — persistent identity with UUID, system_prompt, department_id, triggers, memory 386 2. **Department** — UUID-identified org unit with name, manager_id, parent_id hierarchy 387 3. **Task** — always agent-bound from creation, never free-floating 388 4. **Delegation** — unified delegate tool: message, task, subagent, or broadcast via one interface with 5 response modes (origin, perpetual, async, department, none) 389 390 ### Why This Works 391 392 The subagent delegation model (DelegateTool) already proves the pattern: 393 - Parent spawns child with specific prompt + tools 394 - Child runs independently 395 - Result flows back to parent (sync: inline, async: notification channel) 396 - Depth-limited to prevent recursion 397 398 Persistent agent delegation is the SAME pattern, just with: 399 - Child has its own identity, memory, and tools (from registry) 400 - Result flows back via DelegateResponse dispatch instead of notification channel 401 - Child persists across sessions (entity memory accumulates) 402 - 5 response modes give precise control over where results land 403 404 The supervisor's `create_worker()` already handles the context injection that persistent agents need (identity from registry, blackboard, org context, skills, middleware). The infrastructure is all there — it's just wired through the wrong abstraction (project-based supervisor routing instead of agent-based delegation). 405 406 ### Interaction Model 407 408 **Not every interaction is a task.** The unified delegate tool handles four distinct patterns: 409 410 | Pattern | create_task | What happens | 411 |---------|-------------|-------------| 412 | **Message** | false | Prompt delivered to target, optional response | 413 | **Task delegation** | true | Task lifecycle (Pending → Done/Failed), outcome tracked | 414 | **Subagent** | false | Ephemeral agent spawned inline (to: "subagent") | 415 | **Broadcast** | false | Posted to department, all members' triggers fire | 416 417 **Agent ↔ agent is always async sessions.** Perpetual sessions are for user ↔ agent only. When Agent A messages Agent B, B's trigger fires an async session. B processes the message, responds, session ends. No persistent agent-to-agent sessions. 418 419 **Response routing prevents infinite loops.** Responses carry `reply_to` and do NOT fire the recipient's generic dispatch trigger. They route via the mode specified in the original request (inject into session, spawn purpose-built response session, post to department, or nothing). 420 421 --- 422 423 ## Proposed Architecture 424 425 ### Agent Runtime 426 427 Every persistent agent gets its own worker pool (replaces per-project Supervisor): 428 429 ``` 430 Agent (persistent, has UUID) 431 ├── Identity (system_prompt, persona, memory, skills) 432 ├── Department membership (department_id — which department am I in) 433 ├── Triggers (cron/event/once → spawn async session for THIS agent) 434 ├── Memory (entity-scoped by UUID) 435 ├── Blackboard access (scoped by department membership) 436 │ 437 ├── Can execute tasks itself (agent loop + middleware chain) 438 ├── Unified delegate tool (one tool for all inter-agent interaction): 439 │ ├── Message another agent (response: origin/perpetual/async/department/none) 440 │ ├── Delegate task to another agent (create_task: true) 441 │ ├── Spawn ephemeral subagent (to: "subagent") 442 │ └── Broadcast to department (to: "dept:X") 443 │ 444 └── Worker Pool (infrastructure, not orchestration) 445 ├── max_workers concurrent executions 446 ├── Timeout tracking + reaping 447 ├── Budget enforcement (cost ledger) 448 └── Middleware chain injection 449 450 Department (UUID-identified org unit) 451 ├── name (display label, renameable) 452 ├── manager_id → Agent (nullable, swappable) 453 ├── parent_id → Department (hierarchy) 454 ├── Members: agents WHERE department_id = this 455 ├── Blackboard scope: dept:{uuid}:* 456 ├── ConversationStore: department_chat_id(uuid) for broadcast log 457 └── Escalation: manager handles blocked members, or climbs to parent dept 458 ``` 459 460 ### Task Model 461 462 Tasks are always agent-bound: 463 464 ``` 465 Task { 466 id: String, 467 agent_id: UUID, // WHO owns this — always set, never null 468 subject: String, 469 description: String, 470 status: Pending | InProgress | Done | Blocked | Failed | Cancelled, 471 created_by: Trigger(trigger_id) | Delegation(parent_agent_id) | Self, 472 skill: Option, 473 // ... existing fields (priority, labels, checkpoints, outcome) 474 } 475 ``` 476 477 No task is ever created without an agent_id. The agent_id determines which worker pool runs it, which identity the worker gets, which memory scope to use, and where to escalate. 478 479 ### Unified Delegate Tool 480 481 Today there are three separate tools: `dispatch_send` (message), `delegate` (subagent), and `project_assign` (task). These are the same operation with different parameters. They consolidate into one tool: 482 483 ``` 484 delegate( 485 to: String, // agent name, "dept:Engineering", or "subagent" 486 prompt: String, // what to do / what to say 487 response: "origin" | "perpetual" | "async" | "none", 488 create_task: bool, // track with task lifecycle (default: false) 489 skill: Option, // skill for the target to use 490 tools: Option>, // tool allowlist (subagent only) 491 ) 492 ``` 493 494 **Response modes** — where does the response go: 495 496 | Mode | Where response goes | Session requirement | Use case | 497 |---|---|---|---| 498 | `origin` | Back into the calling session via LoopNotification | Calling session stays alive until response arrives | "I need this answer to continue my current work" | 499 | `perpetual` | Into the calling agent's perpetual session | Agent must have a running perpetual session | "Let me know when it's done, I'll be around" | 500 | `async` | Fresh async session spawned for the calling agent | Always works, no requirement | "Process the result independently with clean context" | 501 | `department` | Into the caller's department ConversationStore | Caller must be in a department | "Share the result with my whole team" | 502 | `none` | Nowhere | Fire and forget | FYI messages, broadcasts, notifications | 503 504 **No infinite loops:** Responses do NOT fire the recipient's generic dispatch trigger. They either inject into a specific session (`origin`/`perpetual`) or spawn a purpose-built response session (`async`) that processes the result and ends — it doesn't auto-reply. 505 506 **Examples:** 507 508 ``` 509 // Fire-and-forget FYI 510 delegate(to: "CPO", prompt: "Deploy freeze Thursday", response: "none") 511 512 // Question — answer comes back here 513 delegate(to: "CPO", prompt: "What's the redesign timeline?", response: "origin") 514 515 // Task delegation — outcome tracked, result in perpetual session 516 delegate(to: "Backend Lead", prompt: "Fix the auth bug", response: "perpetual", create_task: true) 517 518 // Ephemeral subagent — same as today's DelegateTool 519 delegate(to: "subagent", prompt: "Research this API", response: "origin", tools: ["web_fetch", "read_file"]) 520 521 // Department broadcast 522 delegate(to: "dept:Engineering", prompt: "All hands: deploy freeze Thursday", response: "none") 523 524 // Department question — responses come back here from each member 525 delegate(to: "dept:Engineering", prompt: "Status update on your current tasks?", response: "origin") 526 ``` 527 528 **What this replaces:** 529 - `dispatch_send` tool → `delegate(response: "none" | "origin" | ...)` 530 - `delegate` tool (subagent) → `delegate(to: "subagent", ...)` 531 - `project_assign` tool → `delegate(create_task: true, ...)` 532 - `channel_post` / `department_post` → `delegate(to: "dept:X", ...)` 533 534 ### Escalation Model 535 536 Follows department hierarchy (replaces hardcoded escalation_target): 537 538 ``` 539 API Engineer (in dept "Backend") is blocked 540 → Find dept "Backend" → manager is Backend Lead 541 → Backend Lead attempts resolution 542 → Still blocked → find parent dept "Engineering" → manager is CTO 543 → CTO attempts resolution 544 → Still blocked → find parent dept "Executive" → manager is Shadow 545 → Shadow attempts resolution 546 → No parent department → human escalation via gate channels 547 ``` 548 549 ### Departments 550 551 Departments are a lightweight primitive — a UUID-identified organizational unit with its own hierarchy. 552 553 ```sql 554 departments ( 555 id UUID PRIMARY KEY, -- stable identity (all references use this) 556 name TEXT, -- "Engineering" (display label, can be renamed freely) 557 project TEXT, -- which project this department belongs to 558 manager_id UUID NULL, -- FK to agents.id (nullable — can be temporarily unmanaged) 559 parent_id UUID NULL, -- FK to departments.id (department hierarchy) 560 ) 561 562 agents ( 563 id UUID PRIMARY KEY, 564 department_id UUID NULL, -- FK to departments.id (which department am I in) 565 -- NO parent_id on agents anymore — hierarchy lives on departments, not agents 566 ... 567 ) 568 ``` 569 570 **The org hierarchy lives on departments, not agents.** Agents belong to a department. Departments have managers and parent departments. This means: 571 572 - Department UUID is the stable reference — survives manager changes, renames, agent churn 573 - Department name is a display label — rename "Engineering" to "Platform" without breaking anything 574 - Manager is a mutable pointer — swap, remove, or leave vacant without affecting members or history 575 - Blackboard scope is `dept:{department.id}:*` — stable forever 576 - ConversationStore chat_id is `department_chat_id(department.id)` — stable forever 577 578 **Manager vs member separation**: An agent is IN one department (member) and optionally MANAGES another department. The manager belongs to the parent department: 579 580 ``` 581 Department: "Executive" (manager: Shadow, parent: null) 582 Members: CTO, CPO, CFO 583 584 Department: "Engineering" (manager: CTO, parent: "Executive") 585 Members: Backend Lead, Frontend Lead 586 587 Department: "Backend" (manager: Backend Lead, parent: "Engineering") 588 Members: API Engineer, DB Engineer 589 590 Department: "Frontend" (manager: Frontend Lead, parent: "Engineering") 591 Members: UI Engineer, Mobile Engineer 592 ``` 593 594 CTO is IN "Executive" (peers: CPO, CFO). CTO MANAGES "Engineering". Clean separation — you belong to one layer, you manage the layer below. 595 596 **Queries:** 597 - Members of a department: `SELECT * FROM agents WHERE department_id = ?` 598 - Manager of a department: `SELECT manager_id FROM departments WHERE id = ?` 599 - Peers (siblings): agents in the same department (excluding self) 600 - Sub-departments: `SELECT * FROM departments WHERE parent_id = ?` 601 - Parent department: `SELECT parent_id FROM departments WHERE id = ?` 602 603 **Escalation follows department hierarchy:** 604 ``` 605 API Engineer (in "Backend") is blocked 606 → Find department "Backend" → manager is Backend Lead 607 → Backend Lead resolves or escalates 608 → Find parent department "Engineering" → manager is CTO 609 → CTO resolves or escalates 610 → Find parent department "Executive" → manager is Shadow 611 → Shadow resolves or escalates 612 → No parent department → human escalation 613 ``` 614 615 Stable chain. Swap CTO out → update manager_id on "Engineering" → all members, blackboard, conversation history, escalation chain unchanged. 616 617 ### Department Broadcast 618 619 When an operator (or agent) needs to address a whole department: 620 621 ``` 622 Operator posts message to "Engineering" department 623 → Message stored in ConversationStore (keyed by department_chat_id(dept.id) — stable UUID) 624 → Event emitted (DepartmentMessage) 625 → For each agent WHERE department_id = engineering.id: 626 → Agent's event trigger fires (event: department_message) 627 → Async session spawned with: 628 - The broadcast message 629 - Recent department conversation history (from ConversationStore) 630 - Blackboard context (dept:{engineering.id}:* scope) 631 → Agent responds independently in its own session 632 → Response stored in ConversationStore (same department chat_id) 633 → Response shown to operator 634 ``` 635 636 Each agent runs in its own session with its own model and context window. The ConversationStore is the shared record — a message log, not a shared context window. Agents pull recent history when they need it. 637 638 **Preventing cascade loops in department channels:** 639 640 Two types of writes to the department ConversationStore: 641 642 - **Initiating message** (from operator or via delegate tool): stored with `emit_event: true` → DepartmentMessage event fires → member triggers activate 643 - **Agent response** (from a trigger-fired session reacting to a department message): stored with `emit_event: false` → no event, no cascade 644 645 The `record()` method takes an `emit_event` flag. The delegate tool sets it to `true` for new broadcasts. Agent response skills set it to `false`. Responses are stored for history but don't trigger anyone. No infinite loop. 646 647 This covers: 648 - **Operator → department**: user posts, all agents react independently 649 - **Agent → department**: agent posts via delegate tool, members' triggers fire 650 - **Agent response**: stored in channel history, visible to all, but no cascade 651 - **Department history**: ConversationStore keeps the full record, queryable via stable UUID 652 653 ### Escalation to User (Shadow as Inbox) 654 655 When escalation reaches the root department (no parent), it surfaces to the human operator. No separate inbox concept — **Shadow's perpetual session IS the user's inbox.** 656 657 ``` 658 Escalation reaches root department 659 → Root department's manager_id points to Shadow 660 → Escalation injected into Shadow's perpetual session (Telegram/Web/CLI) 661 → Shadow presents to user: "Backend Engineer is blocked on X, needs your input" 662 → User responds to Shadow 663 → Shadow delegates resolution back down: delegate(to: "Backend Engineer", prompt: "User says: ...", response: "none") 664 ``` 665 666 ### Approval / Clarification 667 668 The existing `ClarificationMiddleware` already implements this pattern: 669 670 1. Agent calls `ask_clarification` tool (structured: type, question, context, options) 671 2. Middleware intercepts in `before_tool` hook 672 3. Stores structured request in `WorkerContext::metadata` 673 4. Returns `MiddlewareAction::Halt` — execution stops cleanly 674 5. Task goes to Blocked status 675 6. When answer comes back, task re-queues with the answer 676 677 `ClarificationType` already covers all cases: `MissingInfo`, `Choice`, `Confirmation` (= approval). 678 679 **What changes:** Today, clarification halts and the supervisor escalates to the hardcoded `escalation_target`. In the new architecture, it routes via the department hierarchy instead: 680 681 ``` 682 Agent calls ask_clarification(type: "confirmation", question: "Deploy to prod?", context: "3 files changed") 683 → Middleware halts execution, task goes Blocked 684 → Daemon finds agent's department → department's manager_id 685 → Spawn async session for the department's manager with structured request 686 → Manager responds → answer injected back, task re-queues, agent continues 687 → If no manager (or manager escalates) → climb to parent department → repeat 688 → If root department → surfaces in Shadow's perpetual session → user decides 689 ``` 690 691 No new tool needed. Same `ask_clarification`, same `ClarificationType` enum. Just rewired routing: department chain instead of hardcoded target. The agent doesn't know or care WHO answers — it just asks and gets a response. 692 693 ### Root Architecture 694 695 Shadow is the only agent at the root level. It's the user's proxy in the system. 696 697 ``` 698 Root Department (project: null, manager: Shadow, parent: null) 699 │ 700 ├── "Sigil Core" (project: sigil, manager: CTO, parent: Root) 701 │ ├── "Backend" (project: sigil, manager: BackendLead, parent: Sigil Core) 702 │ │ └── members: API Engineer, DB Engineer 703 │ └── "Frontend" (project: sigil, manager: FrontendLead, parent: Sigil Core) 704 │ └── members: UI Engineer 705 │ 706 └── "Trading" (project: algostaking, manager: TradingLead, parent: Root) 707 └── members: StrategyBot, RiskBot 708 ``` 709 710 - **One root, one inbox**: Shadow's perpetual session is where all unresolved escalations surface 711 - **Projects are a scope tag** on departments (`project` field), not a separate organizational primitive 712 - **Escalation path**: any agent → department manager chain → Shadow → user 713 - **Shadow has unique capabilities**: `spawn_agents`, `spawn_projects`, `create_departments` — it's the only agent that can build the org chart itself 714 715 ### Blackboard Scoping 716 717 Blackboard is the inter-agent knowledge sharing layer (structured, TTL, claims). Separate from ConversationStore (which is the chronological message record). 718 719 Project-scoped blackboard: all agents on a project share knowledge regardless of parent/child. This is for cross-cutting concerns (deploy freezes, shared discoveries, etc.). 720 721 Visibility follows org tree + project scope: 722 723 ``` 724 Root Agent (Shadow) 725 ├── CTO — posts finding:api-redesign → visible to siblings + parent 726 ├── CPO — queries findings → sees CTO's finding:api-redesign 727 └── CFO — posts signal:budget-warning → visible to siblings + parent 728 ``` 729 730 Rules: 731 - Agent can read: own entries, siblings' entries, parent's entries, children's entries, project-scoped entries 732 - Agent can write: own scope only 733 - Claims are visible within parent scope (siblings see each other's claims) 734 - Parent can see all children's entries (read down) 735 - `query_scoped()` already implements this — just needs to be used 736 737 ### DispatchBus Simplification 738 739 Keep only what's needed for agent ↔ agent communication via the unified delegate tool: 740 741 | Kind | Direction | Purpose | 742 |------|-----------|---------| 743 | DelegateRequest | sender → target | Message, task, or department broadcast | 744 | DelegateResponse | target → sender | Reply to a request (routed by response mode) | 745 | HumanEscalation | root dept manager → gate channels | Terminal escalation | 746 747 The delegate tool handles all routing via the `response` parameter (origin/perpetual/async/department/none). DelegateResponse carries a `reply_to` field linking it to the originating request. The daemon routes responses based on the mode specified in the original request. 748 749 Remove: TaskDone, TaskBlocked, TaskFailed, Resolution, PatrolReport, CouncilTopic/Response/Synthesis, AgentAdvice, TaskProposal, DependencySuggestion, WorkerCrashed — all subsumed by DelegateRequest/DelegateResponse. 750 751 ### What Dies 752 753 | Component | Replacement | 754 |-----------|-------------| 755 | `Supervisor` as orchestration engine | Agent's own worker pool (infrastructure only) | 756 | `select_agent_for_task()` | Tasks are agent-bound at creation | 757 | `AgentRole` enum | Removed — every agent can do everything | 758 | `ProjectTeamConfig` | Removed — parent delegates to specific children | 759 | `parent_id` on agents | Replaced by `department_id` — hierarchy lives on departments, not agents | 760 | `department` field (string) on agents | Replaced by `department_id` (UUID FK to departments table) | 761 | ConversationStore as "channel" primitive | Kept as message log for department broadcast + audit only | 762 | `escalation_target` / `system_escalation_target` | Department hierarchy (dept.parent_id → manager_id chain) | 763 | 9 unused DispatchKind variants | Removed | 764 | `PeerAgentConfig` in sigil.toml `[[agents]]` | Agents defined in `agents/*/agent.toml` + agent.md only | 765 | `TeamConfig.agents[]` | Removed — org tree defines relationships | 766 | `TeamConfig.router_model` | Removed — intent classifier already deleted | 767 768 ### What Stays (Unchanged) 769 770 | Component | Why | 771 |-----------|-----| 772 | Agent loop (compaction, tools, notifications) | Core execution engine, production-quality | 773 | DelegateTool mechanics (subagent spawning, notification injection) | Clean, working, depth-limited — unified into new delegate tool | 774 | Middleware chain (9 layers) | Composable, well-tested | 775 | Entity-scoped memory | UUID-keyed, critical for persistent agents | 776 | Blackboard (claims, signals, findings, TTL) | Scoped coordination, well-designed | 777 | Trigger system (cron/event/once) | Agent-owned, advance-before-execute | 778 | DispatchBus (simplified) | Parent ↔ child communication | 779 | Expertise ledger | Agent self-knowledge (not routing) | 780 | Preflight assessment | Agent self-assessment before execution | 781 | Adaptive failure analysis | LLM-based failure classification | 782 | create_worker() context injection | Identity, blackboard, skills, tools, middleware | 783 | IPC protocol | External control plane | 784 | Patrol loop | Heartbeat for trigger firing + worker reaping | 785 | CostLedger, AuditLog | Budget enforcement + decision trail | 786 787 ### What Changes 788 789 | Component | Change | 790 |-----------|--------| 791 | Worker pool | Moves from per-project Supervisor to per-agent | 792 | Escalation | Follows department hierarchy (dept.parent_id → manager_id) instead of hardcoded targets | 793 | Task creation | Requires agent_id (never unassigned) | 794 | Delegation | Unified delegate tool replaces dispatch_send + delegate + project_assign + channel_post | 795 | Response routing | 5 modes (origin, perpetual, async, department, none) replace ad-hoc dispatch handling | 796 | DispatchBus | Simplified to DelegateRequest + DelegateResponse + HumanEscalation | 797 | Org hierarchy | Departments table (UUID, name, manager_id, parent_id) replaces parent_id on agents | 798 | Blackboard queries | Use `query_scoped()` with department-based visibility | 799 | Config | Simplify: remove team routing, role fields; add department definitions | 800 801 ### Simplified Patrol Loop 802 803 ``` 804 Every 30 seconds: 805 1. For each agent with pending tasks: 806 ├─ Reap finished workers 807 ├─ Spawn workers up to max_workers (no routing — agent is known) 808 ├─ Budget enforcement 809 └─ Timeout detection 810 2. Fire due triggers (bind task to trigger's owning agent) 811 3. Handle dispatch delivery (parent ↔ child results) 812 4. Persist state (dispatches, cost) 813 5. Prune expired blackboard + old costs 814 6. Flush memory writes 815 ``` 816 817 No `select_agent_for_task()`. No team matching. No expertise routing for assignment. Just: "this agent has pending tasks → spawn workers with this agent's identity." 818 819 --- 820 821 ## Migration Path 822 823 ### Phase 1: Clean Up Dead Code 824 - Remove `AgentRole` enum (replace match arms, delete field from PeerAgentConfig) 825 - Remove all unused DispatchKind variants (PatrolReport, Council*, AgentAdvice, TaskProposal, DependencySuggestion) 826 - Remove `select_agent_for_task()` body (replace with direct agent_id from task) 827 - Remove `ProjectTeamConfig` and team routing logic 828 - Remove `TeamConfig.agents[]`, `TeamConfig.router_model` 829 830 ### Phase 2: Departments Table 831 - Create `departments` table in agents.db (id UUID, name, project, manager_id, parent_id) 832 - Add `department_id` column to agents table (FK to departments) 833 - Remove `parent_id` and `department` (string) columns from agents 834 - Migrate existing agent relationships into department records 835 - Update AgentRegistry methods: replace parent/children/siblings with department-based queries 836 - Update org context injection to use department hierarchy 837 838 ### Phase 3: Make Tasks Agent-Bound 839 - Add `agent_id: String` to Task struct (required field) 840 - Update task creation paths to always specify agent_id 841 - Update trigger firing to bind task to trigger's owning agent (already mostly does this) 842 - Update IPC `create_task` to require agent_id 843 - Validate: no task can exist with empty agent_id 844 845 ### Phase 4: Replace Supervisor With Agent Worker Pool 846 - Extract worker pool infrastructure from Supervisor into a standalone struct (spawn, track, reap, timeout, budget) 847 - Each agent in the registry gets a worker pool (lazy — only created when agent has pending tasks) 848 - Patrol loop iterates agents with pending tasks, not projects 849 - create_worker() uses agent's own identity from registry (already does this via lookup) 850 - Move context injection logic (blackboard, org context, skills, middleware) to worker pool 851 852 ### Phase 5: Unified Delegate Tool 853 - Consolidate `dispatch_send`, `delegate` (subagent), `project_assign`, `channel_post` into single `delegate` tool 854 - Implement 5 response modes: 855 - `origin` — inject response into calling session via LoopNotification (session kept alive) 856 - `perpetual` — inject into agent's perpetual session 857 - `async` — spawn fresh async session for the sender with the response 858 - `department` — post response to department ConversationStore 859 - `none` — fire and forget 860 - Wire AgentInfra + notification_rx in worker execution path (currently unwired) 861 - Implement DelegateRequest/DelegateResponse dispatch types (replace all existing kinds) 862 - Responses with `reply_to` set do NOT fire triggers — they route via the specified response mode 863 864 ### Phase 6: Escalation Via Department Hierarchy 865 - Replace hardcoded `escalation_target` with department manager chain 866 - On blocked: find agent's department → manager resolves or escalates to parent department's manager 867 - Root department (parent_id = null) manager → human escalation via gate channels 868 - Keep three-strikes tracker, just change the target resolution 869 870 ### Phase 7: Blackboard Scoping 871 - Switch from `query()` to `query_scoped()` (already implemented) 872 - Build AgentVisibility from department membership (department_id, project) 873 - Department members see each other's entries (shared department scope) 874 - Manager can see managed department's entries (read down) 875 - Scope key changes: `dept:{department_uuid}:*` instead of string-based 876 877 ### Phase 8: Rewire Clarification Routing 878 - Existing `ClarificationMiddleware` + `ask_clarification` tool stay unchanged 879 - Rewire routing: instead of hardcoded `escalation_target`, route via department chain 880 - Department chain traversal: find agent's department → manager_id → spawn async session for manager 881 - If no manager or manager escalates → climb to parent department 882 - Root department → inject into Shadow's perpetual session → user decides 883 - No new tool — `ClarificationType::Confirmation` already covers approval 884 885 ### Phase 9: Department Broadcast 886 - Implement DepartmentMessage event type in EventBroadcaster 887 - Wire department_chat_id to use department UUID (stable) 888 - `delegate(to: "dept:X", ...)` posts to ConversationStore + emits DepartmentMessage 889 - Members respond via their `department_message` event triggers (opt-in, per-agent) 890 - IPC command for operator → department broadcast

# Updated Memory Recall
[domain] sg-007-complete-graph-system: sg-007: Complete graph system (2026-03-31). All gaps from the audit closed.

1. **Git commit tracking fixed**: full index now stores last_commit in meta table. Incremental indexing compares against HEAD and only re-parses changed files.

2. **Session primer uses incremental**: emit_graph_status() calls action="incremental" instead of "index". Only re-parses files changed since last indexed commit.

3. **TypeScript language provider**: tree-sitter-typescript. Extracts functions, classes, interfaces, type aliases, enums, methods, properties, arrow functions, variables. Handles .ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx. 3 tests.

4. **Solidity language provider**: tree-sitter-solidity. Extracts contracts, interfaces, libraries, functions, modifiers, events, state variables, structs, enums, imports, inheritance. 1 test.

5. **Cross-project indexing**: all 6 projects indexed:
   - sigil: 264 files, 5,824 nodes (Rust)
   - algostaking: 627 files, 16,876 nodes (Rust)
   - riftdecks-shop: 116 files, 701 nodes (TypeScript)
   - entity-legal: 31 files, 101 nodes (TypeScript)
   - aeqi: 425 files, 5,800 nodes (Solidity)
   - unifutures: 4 files, 11 nodes (Solidity)
   - TOTAL: 1,467 files, 29,313 nodes, 51,345 edges, 536 communities, 306 processes

6. **GraphGuardrailsMiddleware**: new middleware in sigil-orchestrator. Uses sigil-graph to check impact before edit/write tool calls during native agent execution. Injects warnings when file contains symbols with >=10 external callers or traits with >=2 implementations. Order 195 (just before regular guardrails at 200).

Zero python in the entire stack. 589 workspace tests pass.
[domain] sg-017b-cc-grade-compaction: sg-017b: CC-grade compaction upgrades (2026-03-31). 282 lines added to agent.rs.

1. **9-section compaction prompt** (was 6): Primary Intent, Key Technical Concepts, Files+Code (with snippets), Errors+Fixes, Problem Solving (reasoning chain), All User Messages (exact quotes), Pending Tasks, Current Work (file:line precision), Next Step (with direct quotes). NO_TOOLS_PREAMBLE as first instruction. max_tokens 8192 (was 4096).

2. **Post-compact file restoration**: RecentFile struct tracks path+content from file-read tool results. After compaction, top 5 most recent files re-injected (POST_COMPACT_MAX_TOKENS_PER_FILE=5000, POST_COMPACT_FILE_BUDGET=50000). Prevents re-reading files already examined.

3. **Conversation repair** (repair_tool_pairing): Before each API call, ensures tool_use/tool_result bidirectional pairing. Dangling tool_uses get synthetic error results. Orphan tool_results stripped. Empty tool messages removed. Prevents API 400 after compaction drops messages.

4. **Diminishing returns detection**: Tracks consecutive iterations with <500 completion tokens (DIMINISHING_RETURNS_THRESHOLD). After 3+ (DIMINISHING_RETURNS_COUNT), halts with AgentStopReason::Halted. Prevents infinite low-value loops.

These close the remaining quality gaps between Sigil's compaction and Claude Code's. The main remaining CC advantage is prompt caching (system prompt split with global/org cache scopes) and ContentReplacementState (frozen tool result persistence decisions for byte-identical re-application).
[domain] claude-code-compaction-prompt-reference: Claude Code's 9-section compaction prompt (reference for Sigil implementation). Source: /tmp/claude-agent-sdk/src/services/compact/prompt.ts

The prompt has 3 variants:
1. BASE_COMPACT_PROMPT — full conversation summary
2. PARTIAL_COMPACT_PROMPT (direction='from') — summarize recent messages only
3. PARTIAL_COMPACT_UP_TO_PROMPT (direction='up_to') — summarize prefix, newer messages follow

All variants:
- Start with NO_TOOLS_PREAMBLE ("CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY. Do NOT call any tools.")
- End with NO_TOOLS_TRAILER (reminder that tool calls will be rejected)
- Include DETAILED_ANALYSIS_INSTRUCTION (requires <analysis> block for thinking)
- Support custom instructions appended (user-configured or from PreCompact hooks)

The 9 sections (BASE):
1. Primary Request and Intent — all user requests and intents in detail
2. Key Technical Concepts — technologies and frameworks discussed
3. Files and Code Sections — file paths, code snippets, why important, changes made
4. Errors and Fixes — errors encountered, how fixed, user feedback
5. Problem Solving — solved problems and ongoing troubleshooting
6. All User Messages — ALL non-tool-result user messages (critical for intent tracking)
7. Pending Tasks — explicitly asked tasks
8. Current Work — precise description of what was being worked on immediately before summary
9. Optional Next Step — with direct quotes from recent conversation to prevent drift

formatCompactSummary() strips <analysis> tags and formats <summary> tags.

getCompactUserSummaryMessage() wraps the summary with:
- "This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context."
- Link to full transcript file for details
- Whether recent messages are preserved verbatim
- Auto-continue instruction if suppressFollowUpQuestions=true

Post-compact restoration (compact.ts:532-585):
- createPostCompactFileAttachments: top 5 recent files (sorted by timestamp, 5K tokens each, 50K total)
- createSkillAttachmentIfNeeded: invoked skills (sorted by recency, 5K/skill, 25K total)
- createPlanAttachmentIfNeeded: plan file content
- createPlanModeAttachmentIfNeeded: plan mode instructions
- createAsyncAgentAttachmentsIfNeeded: running/completed agent status
- getDeferredToolsDeltaAttachment: re-announce all deferred tools
- getAgentListingDeltaAttachment: re-announce agent definitions
- getMcpInstructionsDeltaAttachment: re-announce MCP server instructions
- processSessionStartHooks: re-run session start hooks
